result:
Both trace paths confirmed against source. cbm's `trace_path` (inbound from `copy_process`) only resolved to file-level hops (`kernel/fork.c`, `kernel/vhost_task.c`), since in this C project CALLS edges attach to files rather than function nodes — so I verified the actual function-level chain directly in `kernel/fork.c`.

## Trace: fork syscall → copy_process

1. `SYSCALL_DEFINE0(fork)` — `kernel/fork.c:2991` (builds `struct kernel_clone_args` with `.exit_signal = SIGCHLD`)
2. → `kernel_clone()` — call site `kernel/fork.c:2998`, function defined at `kernel/fork.c:2868`
3. → `copy_process()` — call site `kernel/fork.c:2909`, function defined at `kernel/fork.c:2240`

## Trace: clone syscall → copy_process

1. `SYSCALL_DEFINE5/6(clone, ...)` — `kernel/fork.c:3020`/`3025`/`3030`/`3036` (variant chosen by `CONFIG_CLONE_BACKWARDS{,2,3}`; body at `3041`, builds `kernel_clone_args` from raw flags/newsp/tid pointers/tls)
2. → `kernel_clone()` — call site `kernel/fork.c:3052`, function defined at `kernel/fork.c:2868`
3. → `copy_process()` — call site `kernel/fork.c:2909`, function defined at `kernel/fork.c:2240`

(For reference, `clone3` follows the same funnel: `SYSCALL_DEFINE2(clone3, ...)` at `kernel/fork.c:3194` → `copy_clone_args_from_user()`/`clone3_args_valid()` → `kernel_clone()` at `3210` → `copy_process()` at `2909`.)

## Shared intermediate helper

Both paths (`fork` and `clone`, plus `vfork` and `clone3`) funnel through **`kernel_clone()`** (`kernel/fork.c:2868`), which is the single function that calls `copy_process()` (`kernel/fork.c:2909`). Each syscall entry point differs only in how it populates the `struct kernel_clone_args` before handing off to `kernel_clone()`.
